summary:
Prior radiation therapy significantly increases the risk of developing non-Hodgkkin’s lymphoma, particularly diffusely large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), due to induced genomic instability and DNA damage in lymphocytes. This mechanism aligns well with both biologic plausibility and existing oncology literature despite limited specific studies directly confirming DLBCL post-radiotherapy outcomes.  

Note: No mention of previous breast cancer being causative; thus, option C remains best-supported based on radiobiological pathways.

Note: No mention of previous breast cancer being causative; thus, option C remains best-supported based on radiobiological pathways.. Answer choice: C
